features.summary: Delta reported Q2 2021 revenues of $6.3 billion, down 49% versus 2019, representing a 76% sequential improvement from Q1. The company achieved profitability in June with high single-digit pretax margins despite missing 40% of 2019 revenue levels. Delta generated $1.5 billion in free cash flow and narrowed its pretax loss to $881 million for the quarter. The airline is hiring 4,000-5,000 employees and expects mid-single-digit pretax margins in Q3 as demand continues recovering.
features.management_outlook: Management expects Q3 total revenue to recover 65-70% versus 2019 on 70-72% capacity recovery, representing another $2 billion sequential revenue increase. Domestic corporate volumes are expected to reach 55-60% recovery by end of Q3, up from 40% in Q2. The company anticipates profitability in both Q3 and Q4 quarters and expects to achieve non-fuel CASM below 2019 levels by Q4. International recovery is accelerating with strong bookings to reopened European countries, though Pacific markets remain challenging.
features.risks: New COVID variants pose risks to recovery pace, particularly affecting regions with low vaccination rates. International demand recovery remains choppy and uneven, dependent on border reopenings and vaccination progress globally. The company faces operational challenges from rapid demand surge, including extended customer service wait times and staffing pressures. Inflationary pressures from vendors and accelerated hiring of frontline employees create cost headwinds, with rebuild expenses expected at 5-6 points in both Q3 and Q4.
features.key_drivers: Domestic leisure demand and yields have exceeded June 2019 levels, driving strong revenue recovery momentum. Corporate travel is accelerating with 95% of accounts indicating office returns by year-end and 93% planning increased Q3 travel. The Delta-American Express co-brand program shows remarkable resilience with June card spend at 115% of 2019 levels despite travel purchases still down 25%. Premium product demand is outpacing main cabin by 5-10 points, supporting the company's de-commoditization strategy and brand strength.
